title: ageratina prunifolia
intro: Ageratina prunifolia, also known as Mexican tea or Cimarron tea, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It is a shrub-like plant with medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which also offer health benefits such as reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. Ageratina prunifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it easily accessible for herbal use.
